info.title: Log Analysis API
info.description:
*REST API to send log data, export data and manage configuration*

To utilize the inline testing functionality here, please go into your Mezmo account and add the following
address to your CORS Origins page: `https://docs.mezmo.com`.

tags.name: Archiving
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The LogDNA Archiving API enables you to programmatically configure your archiving instances via code.
Specifically you can set up cold storage buckets to send your logs for long term storage.

tags.name: Board
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The LogDNA's API's Board endpoints are used to create, get, list, and delete boards.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The LogDNA API's Configuration endpoints are used to create, update, and delete views, alerts, and categories.

tags.name: Export
tags.description:
LogDNA provides two endpoints for exporting your log lines.
Read the sections below to learn about each, and determine which is appropriate for your use case.

## Export API v1
The Export API v1 is used to export log lines in [JSONL](https://jsonlines.org/) format. Based on
your specific plan, the maximum number of logs returned is limited to 10,000 / 20,000. There are two distinct functionalities:
1. Streaming desired log lines directly from the response as raw text data
2. Specifying an email to receive a link that can be used to the download the desired log lines

> Note
> When an email address is specified, the desired log lines will not be streamed to the response body.
> The response will instead send a notification indicating that the request was successful and that the results
> will be sent to the email address provided in the request. This entails archiving and uploading the desired logs
> to a storage provider. The email includes a URI to download the logs. Be aware that access to this URI will expire
> after 24-48 hours.

## Export API v2
The Export API v2 provides an enhancement over v1, allowing you to export any number of log lines using pagination and
by-passing the plan-specific limit imposed in v1. By making (multiple) subsequent requests using the `pagination_id` parameter,
you can retrieve logs in batches of JSON until all matching log lines have been returned.

Each Export API v2 request is limited to 10,000 lines.

### Pagination Functionality
Pagination is only necessary when the desired set of log lines is larger than 10,000, since this is the maximum number of results
that can be returned in a given page. If an export does not exceed this size, the behavior is similar to v1 -- all of the logs will be
returned in a single "page" and no further requests are needed. When an export does exceed this size, refer to the steps outlined below.

This is the expected workflow to export a set (cardinality > 10,000) of log lines that requires pagination:
1. Send an initial request with valid query parameters to retrieve logs. Pass a `null` value for the `pagination_id` in the first request.
2. The JSON response contains 2 fields: `lines` and `pagination_id`. The former is an array containing the corresponding batch (the first
   batch in this case) of logs and the latter is a token used to retrieve the next page of results.
3. Send a subsequent request using the same initial query parameters and also passing in the token received in the response to the `pagination_id`
   parameter. This will retrieve the next page of results.
4. Repeat this request-response cycle, updating the `pagination_id` parameter with the token returned in every new response, until the
   response eventually returns a `null` value for the token. This indicates that the corresponding batch of logs is the last and that you have
   reached the final page.

> Important Considerations
> In subsequent requests to paginate through results, make sure none of the query parameters & values are modified from the initial request
> (excluding `pagination_id`)

> The tokens used in the `pagination_id` parameter are tied to a specific export and page so they can be used repeatedly in requests to return
> deterministic results

> "0" can be passed in to the `from` and `to` parameters which will be resolved to timestamps for a plan's retention boundary and the current
> time respectively. This behavior is independent so the value can be passed interchangeably to the parameters -- resulting in 4 possible scenarios:
> - [`from`: \<user-specified timestamp\>, `to`: \<user-specified timestamp\>]
> - [`from`: 0 (retention boundary), `to`: \<user-specified timestamp\>]
> - [`from`: \<user-specified timestamp\>, `to`: 0 (current time)]
> - [`from`: 0 (retention boundary), `to`: 0 (current time)]

tags.name: Exclusions
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

Control what you store by creating exclusion rules. New lines that match an exclusion rule will not be stored and will not count toward your storage quota.

tags.name: Suspensions
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The LogDNA Start/Stop Ingestion API allows users to programmatically manage their data ingestion status.

Users can get the current status of their ingestion, suspend their ingestion, and resume their ingestion. To prevent an
accidental API call, the suspension of ingestion requires two subsequent API calls.

> You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

tags.name: Usage v1
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The Usage API provides endpoints for retrieving aggregated usage information for applications, hosts and tags during a time period.

While the `to` and `from` times are unix timestamps, the report is granular by day.  Therefore only the day portion is used to establish a date range.

### How is **Usage** defined?

This API defines usage as a percentage of the total number of lines ingested by the account within the defined period of time. For
example, an app called `myapp` that returns a percentage of `15` in the response object has 15 percent of their logs coming from `myapp`.

tags.name: Usage v2
tags.description:
>*Authentication*: You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).

The Usage API provides endpoints for retrieving aggregated usage information for applications, hosts and tags during a time period.

While the `to` and `from` times are unix timestamps, the report is granular by day.  Therefore only the day portion is used to establish a date range.

### How is **Usage** defined?

This API defines usage as the number of bytes used by the log lines stored
on disk by the account within the defined period of time. For example,
an app called `myapp` that returns `150,000` total bytes in the response
object indicates that `myapp` is consuming `150,000` bytes of disk storage
over the time range.

post.description:
Use this method to send logs to a logging instance.

### Authentication
You can find instructions on authentication [here](/log-analysis-api/ref#authentication).  Ingestion is
similar to the other APIs, but instead of `servicekey` you will use `apikey` if
you are using the header style authentication.

### Metadata
Meta is a field reserved for custom information associated with a log line. To add 
metadata to an API call, specify the `meta` field under the lines object. Metadata can be
viewed inside that line's context

WARNING: If inconsistent value types are
used, that line's metadata, will not be parsed. For example, if a line is passed
with a meta object, such as `meta.myfield` of type String, any subsequent lines
with `meta.myfield` must have a String as the value type for `meta.myfield`.
> Please be aware of [service limits](https://docs.mezmo.com/docs/Mezmo-ingestion-service-limits)
> on this endpoint

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: Host name of the source.

parameters.name: mac
parameters.description: The network mac address of the host computer.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: The local IP address of the host computer.

parameters.name: now
parameters.description: The source UNIX timestamp in milliseconds at the time of the request. Used to calculate time drift.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tags that are used to dynamically group hosts.

requestBody.description: You can send multiple log lines in a request.

get.description: Use this method to export logs in JSON format from a logging instance.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start time. Set as UNIX timestamp in seconds or milliseconds.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End time. Set as UNIX timestamp in seconds or milliseconds.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of log lines to include in the export.

parameters.name: hosts
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of hosts.

parameters.name: apps
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of applications.

parameters.name: levels
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of log levels.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query.

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: Defines the log lines that you want to export. Valid values are head, first log lines, and tail, last log lines. If not specified, defaults to tail.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Specifies the email with the downloadable link of your export. By default, the log lines are streamed.

parameters.name: emailSubject
parameters.description: Use to set the subject of the email. Use to represent a space. For example, a sample value is Export logs.

parameters.name: from

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End time (inclusive). Set as UNIX timestamp in seconds or milliseconds.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of log lines to include in the export.

parameters.name: hosts
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of hosts.

parameters.name: apps
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of applications.

parameters.name: levels
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of log levels.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query.

parameters.name: prefer
parameters.description: Defines the log lines that you want to export. Valid values are head, first log lines, and tail, last log lines. If not specified, defaults to tail.

parameters.name: pagination_id
parameters.description: ID that indicates which page of results to be retrieved. Leave empty for the initial export request.

get.description: Get aggregated usage information for an account's data during a time period.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start time. A date-time string as defined by the ISO 8601 date format. This date-time will be used to calculate the starting day; the exact time will not be used.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End time. A date-time string as defined by the ISO 8601 date format. This date-time will be used to calculate the ending day; the exact time will not be used.

error.description: Message indicating why the request is bad.

get.description: Lists aggregated usage information for all apps during a time period.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start time. A date-time string as defined by the ISO 8601 date format.  This date-time will be used to calculate the starting day; the exact time will not be used.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End time. A date-time string as defined by the ISO 8601 date format.  This date-time will be used to calculate the ending day; the exact time will not be used.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum amount of apps to retrieve.

parameters.name: retention
parameters.description: Limit report to apps that contribute to a variable retention pool. Value must be a string indicating the retention pool duration, e.g. `7d` for a seven day retention pool.

from.description: The calculated starting time (exclusive) used internally.

to.description: The calculated ending time (exclusive) used internally.

total_bytes.description: The number of bytes of disk the app consumes over the time range.

error.description: Message indicating why the request is bad.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of all the keys
get.description: Retrieve a list of all the keys for an account. Supported key types include `ingestion` and `service`. Use the `type` parameter to filter which type of keys to retrieve.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of key to retrieve. Defaults to "all"

200.description: A list of keys is returned on success.

400.description: The supplied request is invalid or malformed.

example.error: "type" must be one of [ingestion, service, all]

post.description: Create a new key of the type specified by the `type` parameter. If a name is not supplied, one will be auto generated.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of key to be created

requestBody.description: Modifiable fields can be set in the request body.

200.description: A new key was created successfully! The entire key resource is returned.

400.description: The supplied request is invalid or malformed.

example.error: "type" is required

get.description: Retrieves a specific key by its `id`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the key to retrieve

200.description: The key with the supplied `id` was found, the entire resource is returned.

404.description: The specified key was not found.

example.error: Key with id 629eb5369e5b231f51d3830f was not found

delete.description: Delete a specific key by its `id`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the key to delete

200.description: The key was deleted successfully, a simple status is returned.

message.description: The status of the request in a human-readable form

deleted.description: Indicates whether the key was deleted

404.description: The specified key was not found.

example.error: Key with id 629eb5369e5b231f51d3830f was not found

put.summary: Update one or more fields of a key resource
put.description: Updates all of the fields described in the request body of the specified key. Fields left out of the body will remain unaffected.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the key to update

200.description: The update was successful; the key resource is returned with updated fields.

400.description: The supplied request is invalid or malformed.

example.error: "name" length must be less than or equal to 30 characters long

404.description: The specified key was not found.

example.error: Key with id 629eb5369e5b231f51d3830f was not found

get.description: Retrieves all of the current members in an organization and returns their email, role, and groups.

200.description: A list of members is returned on success.

post.description: Create a new user and add to the team by providing 'email', 'role' and an optional list of RBAC groups.

200.description: A new member was created successfully! Entire memberResource is returned.

400.description: The supplied request is invalid or malformed.

example.error: "role" is required

409.description: The supplied request leads to an invalid state. The returned error will contain more helpful information.

example.error: Admins can\'t be assigned to any groups

get.description: Returns the role and groups of the specified member.

200.description: The member with the supplied `email` was found, the entire resource is returned.

404.description: The specified member was not found.

example.error: Member "fakeaccount@mezmo.com" not found

delete.description: Removes a member from the organization. This does not delete the user.

200.description: The member was removed successfully, a simple status is returned.

message.description: The status of the request in a human-readable form

deleted.description: Indicates whether the member was deleted

404.description: The specified member was not found.

example.error: The provided email is not an active member of this account.

409.description: The specified member (owner) cannot be removed

example.error: The owner of the account can\'t be removed.

put.description: Updates all the fields described in the request body of the specified member. Creates a member if the member doesn't exist already. Fields left out of the body will remain unaffected.

200.description: The update was successful; the member resource is returned with updated fields.

400.description: The supplied request is invalid or malformed.

example.error: "value" must contain at least one of [role, groups]

409.description: The supplied request leads to an invalid state. The returned error will contain more helpful information.

example.error: Admins can\'t be assigned to any groups

get.description: Retrieves a list of all child organizations that belong to the enterprise.

200.description: A list of child organizations is returned on success.

400.description: A poorly formatted request returns with a `BadRequest` code.

example.error: "test" is not allowed

post.summary: Attach a child organization to an enterprise
post.description: Attaches an existing organization to an enterprise. The `retention` and `owner` field are optional, and will be defaulted if not specified. To specify which child organization to attach, one of its service keys must be supplied through our typical service key authorization schema; either as basic authorization or through the `servicekey` header.

400.description: A poorly formatted request returns with a `BadRequest` code.

example.error: Service Key Validation Error: Invalid or deactivated servicekey

409.description: The supplied request leads to an invalid state. The returned error will contain more helpful information.

example.error: No contract found for this enterprise.

get.summary: Get details of a child organization
get.description: Retrieves a specific child organization by its id. If the organization does not belong to the enterprise, a 404 error is returned.

200.description: The child organization was found and returned.

put.description: Makes an update to the specified child organization. Used to modify the current retention with the `retention` field or the current owner with the `owner` field.

200.description: The account was found, the enterpriseId and retention details are updated.

example.error: "plan" is not allowed

404.description: Unable to update account which not belong to enterprise

example.error: Invalid Account.

409.description: Return error when account does not have contract tier

example.error: No contract found for this account.

delete.summary: Detach a child org from Enterprise
delete.description: Detaches a child organization from the enterprise. The child organization is not deleted and can continue to work afterwards.

200.description: The child org was removed successfully, a simple status is returned.

example.error: "account_id" is not allowed
Invalid Account

200.description: The Enterprise ServiceKey was deleted successfully, a simple status is returned.

deleted.description: Indicates whether the member was deleted

A new screen was created successfully! Entire memberResource is returned.

404.description: The specified screen was not found.

Database operation failed to get stream configuration.

from.description: The calculated starting time (exclusive) used internally.

to.description: The calculated ending time (exclusive) used internally.

xd_vr_bytes.description: The number of bytes of logs stored for this variable  retention type where x is 3, 7, 14, or 30 day

from.description: The calculated starting time (exclusive) used internally.

to.description: The calculated ending time (exclusive) used internally.

password.description: Password provided in credentials for authorization

mac.description: The network mac address of the host computer.

ip.description: The local IP address of the host computer (ipv4 or ipv6)

now.description: Field format in date-time notation. The source UNIX timestamp in milliseconds at the time of the request. Used to calculate time drift.

tags.description: Tags that are used to dynamically group hosts.

timestamp.description: UNIX timestamp, including milliseconds, when the log entry was recorded.

app.description: Name of the application that generates the log line.

level.description: Set a value for the level. For example, sample values for this parameter are INFO, WARNING, ERROR.

meta.description: This field is reserved for custom information that is associated with a log line. To add metadata to an API call, specify the meta field under the lines object. Metadata can be viewed inside that line's context.

from.description: Start time. Set as UNIX timestamp in seconds or milliseconds.

to.description: End time. Set as UNIX timestamp in seconds or milliseconds.

size.description: Number of log lines to include in the export.

prefer.description: Defines the log lines that you want to export. Valid values are head, first log lines, and tail, last log lines. If not specified, defaults to tail.

email.description: Specifies the email with the downloadable link of your export. By default, the log lines are streamed.

ownerEmail.description: The email of a user to be given owner privileges of an account.

emailSubject.description: Use to set the subject of the email. Use to represent a space. For example, a sample value is Export logs.

integration.description: Defines the type of notification channel. Valid values are: `email`, `webhook`, `slack`, and `pagerduty`

archiveintegration.description: Defines the type of archive destination. Valid values are: `ibm`, `s3`, `gcs`, `dos`, `azblob`, and `swift`

pagination_id.description: ID that indicates which page of results to be retrieved. Leave empty for the initial export request.

triggerlimit.description: Specify the number of log lines that match the view's filtering and search criteria. When the number of log lines is reached, an alert is triggered.

triggerinterval.description: Specify how often to trigger an alert. Valid values are: 30 seconds, 1 minute, 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 6 hours, 12 hours, 24 hours

autoresolve.description: Set to true if you want the set a condition to resolve the incident that was raised by this alert.  This property only applies to PagerDuty.

autoresolvelimit.description: Specify the number of log lines that match the view's filtering and search criteria. When the number of log lines is reached, this incident will be set to resolved in PagerDuty.

autoresolveinterval.description: Interval of time to aggregate and check # of matched lines against the auto resolve limit. Valid values are: 30 seconds, 1 minute, 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 6 hours, 12 hours, 24 hours, 25 hours.

immediate.description: Set to true if you want the trigger condition to be evaluated as soon as the triggerlimit is reached.

terminal.description: Set to true if you want the trigger condition to be evaluated after the time that you specify in the triggerinterval field is reached.

operator.description: Type of alert. Valid values are: `presence`and `absence`.

timezone.description: Timezone used to report timestamps. This can be any value defined in the tz database https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones.

category.description: Logical name that you can use to group views.

accessscope.description: Query that determines the data that is visible to members of a group.

keyValue.description: A secret key which can be used to authenticate requests to Mezmo.

keyCreated.description: The time the key was created as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds

keyName.description: A user friendly name for the key

role.description: role of member. Owner shall not be created or set through api

rbacGroupId.description: The ID of one of RBAC groups that belong to the account

enterpriseId.description: Unique Id of the enterprise organization

retention.description: Current retention period selected for the child organization

retentionPeriods.description: Available log retention options for the child organization

brokers.description: List of URLs referencing Kafka broker connections via SASL_SSL

topic.description: Name of the relevant Kafka topic category storing log records

stream_user.description: Username in provided credentials to authorize access to brokers

stream_password.description: Password in provided credentials to authorize access to brokers

status.description: Enumerated (binary) string representing whether or not the stream config is active

streamConfigRequest.description: Contains settings needed to set up connection(s) to Kafka brokers

max_lines.description: The number of lines required in order to trigger the alert.

max_z_score.description: The number of standard deviations above the 30 day average lines in order to trigger the alert.

threshold_alert.description: Determines if you want alerts to be triggered if both the max lines and standard deviation have been triggered, or if one of the thresholds has been reached.

frequency.description: Notify recipients once per hour, or once per day, (starting when the threshold is passed the first time) until the index rate drops below the thresholds. When the index rate drops below the thresholds, alerting stops.

max_lines.description: The number of lines required in order to trigger the alert.

max_z_score.description: The number of standard deviations above the 30 day average lines in order to trigger the alert.

threshold_alert.description: Determines if you want alerts to be triggered if both the max lines and standard deviation have been triggered, or if one of the thresholds has been reached.

frequency.description: Notify recipients once per hour, or once per day, (starting when the threshold is passed the first time) until the index rate drops below the thresholds. When the index rate drops below the thresholds, alerting stops.

errCode.description: Entity used as an arbitrary classifier of the error encountered

errMsg.description: Error information surfaced to end user(s) in the response

message.description: Message providing context on the rule / property that failed validation

key.description: Relevant property or rule in the schema associated with failure message

errValidation.description: Specific error details related to invalid / missing request parameters

integrationArchiving.description: Set to `ibm` to archive to IBM Cloud Object Storage

endpoint.description: Private endpoint associated with the bucket.

apikey.description: API key that is needed to authenticate with the Cloud Object Storage service.

resourceinstanceid.description: CRN of the Cloud Object Storage instance ID where the bucket is available.

error.description: A human-readable description of the error

code.description: A unique code for the type of error

account.description: The account this screen belongs to.

ratio.description: The width to height aspect ratio of this screen.

rotate90Degrees.description: Whether the screen is rotated 90 degrees. Setting to true effectively swaps the width to height ratio.

background.description: The background color to use when viewing the screen.

categories.description: A list of string categories the screen belongs to.

operation.description: The type of operation to perform on the field indicated in the “field” option. If the "field" option is set to all lines, only "count" can be used and other values may not display any results.

duration.description: How far into the past to graph this widget. Time shifted graph widgets must use time shifted durations e.g. "iso-weekly-compare". All other widgets must use regular durations e.g. "hourly".

field.description: Which field is being operated on and displayed in this widget. Use the special field “__ALL__LINES__” to perform the operation on all lines.

match.description: A special matching operation done on each value of the field. If the field is a string type then this value is used for equivalence matching. For number types, the supported operators are ["*", ">N", "<N", "=N", ">=N", "<=N"]. If the field is set the special type "__ALL_LINES__", this option is disabled.

filter.description: An extra query filter to run to match lines. Must be specified as a Mezmo line query e.g. “statusCode:>=400”.

label.description: A label that is displayed on the widget.

start.description: The top left corner of the widget as an [x, y] coordinate.

end.description: The bottom right corner of the widget as an [x, y] coordinate.

position.description: The positional coordinates of a widget. Screens coordinates start at at the top left at [1, 1] and increase down and to the right of a screen.

sort.description: The order in which the rows of a table are sorted. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

rows.description: How many rows to include for the table. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

leftLabel.description: Label to display on the left side of a table. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

rightLabel.description: Label to display on the right side of a table. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

showBarChart.description: Whether the rows of a table should also be displayed as a bar chart. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

groupBy.description: An additional field with which to group results. This option is specific to the "table" widget.

min.description: The minimum bounds of the gauge. This option is specific to the "gauge" widget.

max.description: The maximum bounds of the gauge. This option is specific to the "gauge" widget.

units.description: A label for the units of this widget. This option is specific to the "count" and "gauge" widgets.

format.description: How to format the results of this widget. This option is specific to the "count", "gauge", and "table" widgets.

account.description: The account this screen belongs to.

AccessToken.name: Authorization
AccessToken.description:
This key is used for all apis with the exception of ingest.  You can obtain it following
[these instructions](https://docs.mezmo.com/docs/ingestion-key) and placing
the key in the header of your call in the format of `Authorization: Token {accesstoken}`
For example:
```
curl -H 'Authorization: Token <ACCESS TOKEN>'
```

servicekeyAuth.name: servicekey
servicekeyAuth.description:
(Deprecated) This key is used for v1 and v2 apis with the exception of ingest.
Service keys can be used by placing your service key in the `Authorization`
header of your api call. *NOTE* Usage of the service key auth mechanism has been deprecated
in favor of IAM Access Tokens. Service keys can no longer be created, but can still be
used for v1 and v2 apis while the transition takes place

enterpriseServiceKeyAuth.name: enterprise-servicekey
enterpriseServiceKeyAuth.description:
When authorizing against our enterprise endpoints, they expect an enterprise service key
instead of a regular organization service key. Some endpoints might expect both an
enterprise service key and a regular organization service key.

apikeyAuth.name: apikey
apikeyAuth.description:
(Deprecated) This key is used for ingestion.  You can obtain it following
[these instructions](https://docs.mezmo.com/docs/ingestion-key) and placing
the key in the header of your call. *NOTE* Usage of the apikey auth mechanism has been deprecated
in favor of IAM Access Tokens. Service keys can no longer be created, but can still be
used for v1 and v2 apis while the transition takes place

basicAuth.description:
(Deprecated) As an alternative to authenticating with a header value, you can also use basic authentication.
To use either `apiKey` or `servicekey` as basic authentication, simply pass the key
as the username with no password.  For example:
```
curl https://api.logdna.com/v1/config/view -u INSERT_API_KEY:
```
